artifacts.summary: Research on making extension verification syncprotect-aware: path format analysis, shared helper extraction plan, detect_legacy_core fix approach, and specific line-level change locations
Implementation plan with 3 phases: verify.lua syncprotect support, init.lua call site wiring and detect_legacy_core fix, end-to-end validation

active_projects.completion_summary: Made verify.lua syncprotect-aware by adding protected_paths parameter to verify_rules(), verify_context(), and verify_extension(). Fixed detect_legacy_core() in init.lua to filter against the core manifest's agent list, preventing extension-managed agents from being falsely flagged as legacy core artifacts.

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive analysis of Neovim and WezTerm configurations showing no automatic directory-changing behavior
Research report documenting OAuth2 token expiration as root cause and fix procedure
3-phase implementation plan for fixing Gmail OAuth2 authentication

Full architecture walkthrough: CR handler code, special entries pattern, extension manager API (reload exists, reload_all does not), safe reload ordering, and specific proposed code changes for both features
2-phase plan: submenu for loaded extensions (Unload/Reload/Cancel) and [Reload All] special entry with dependency-safe reload loop
Both phases completed: vim.ui.select submenu for loaded extensions and [Reload All] entry with dependency-safe reload loop

active_projects.description: Add reload option to <leader>al extension picker. Phase 1: When <CR> on a loaded extension, show Unload/Reload/Cancel submenu instead of directly unloading. Phase 2: Add [Reload All] special entry above [Keyboard Shortcuts] to wipe and reload all currently loaded extensions and core system. Files: picker/init.lua (CR handler + reload-all action), display/entries.lua (new special entry).
active_projects.completion_summary: Added reload functionality to the extension picker: (1) pressing CR on a loaded extension now shows a vim.ui.select submenu with Unload/Reload/Cancel options wrapped in vim.schedule; (2) a new [Reload All] special entry reloads all loaded extensions in dependency-safe order (non-core first, core last) with error collection and summary notification. Both changes are scoped to picker/init.lua and picker/display/entries.lua.

artifacts.summary: Current state analysis of all 3 lean extension files, gap analysis identifying lean_verify as missing, 8 specific proposed changes with exact before/after text for implementing LSP-first verification policy
3-phase implementation plan with 8 targeted text edits across 3 lean extension files to implement LSP-first verification policy
All 3 phases completed: lean_verify added to tool tables, three-tier verification cadence documented across all files, lean_multi_attempt positioned as pre-edit trial

active_projects.description: Update lean extension to prefer lean-lsp MCP tools over lake build for per-step verification. Add lean_verify to tool docs, replace per-step lake build with lean_goal, position lean_multi_attempt as pre-edit trial, prefer lake build Module.Name over bare lake build, reserve full lake build for phase-end and final verification only. Files: lean4.md rules, lean-implementation-agent.md, lean-implementation-flow.md.
active_projects.completion_summary: Updated three lean extension files to implement LSP-first verification policy: added lean_verify to all tool tables, replaced per-step lake build with lean_goal + lean_verify across all three files, positioned lean_multi_attempt as pre-edit trial step, established three-tier cadence (per-step: lean_goal+lean_verify, phase-end: scoped lake build, final: full lake build).

artifacts.summary: Step C Python regex matches legacy format (- #N:) that never exists in TODO.md. Actual format is ### N. Title blocks. Fix: replace with block removal anchored on ^### {N}\. pattern.
2-phase plan to fix broken Step C regex in archive-task.sh and sync extension copy
Fixed Step C regex, synced extension copy, verified with 4 test cases

active_projects.description: The archive-task.sh script moves task directories to archive/ and updates state.json (removing from active_projects, adding to completed_projects) but does NOT remove the corresponding task entry block from TODO.md. This leaves stale [COMPLETED] entries in TODO.md after /todo runs. Fix: add a sed/awk step to archive-task.sh that removes the full task entry block (from ### {N}. to the next --- or ### delimiter) from TODO.md after archiving. Also update the extension copy at .claude/extensions/core/scripts/archive-task.sh.
active_projects.completion_summary: Fixed archive-task.sh Step C by replacing a Python regex that matched a non-existent '- #N:' format with a line-by-line block removal anchored on '^### N. ' to match the actual TODO.md block format. Both the primary script and extension copy were updated and verified identical.
